channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A Lebanese man who spent 32 years in Syrian prisons is welcomed home
item.description: CHEKKA, Lebanon (AP) — Suheil Hamwi spent 32 years in a Syrian prison, and now, after an offensive by insurgents that toppled the government of Bashar Assad, he's finally returned to his home in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: San Diego sheriff defies new policy to limit cooperation with immigration officials
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The sheriff of the nation’s fifth-largest county on Tuesday defied a new policy to limit cooperation with federal immigration authorities, setting up a showdown over a new obstacle to President-elect Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A Malibu wildfire prompts evacuation orders and warnings for 20,000, including Dick Van Dyke, Cher
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Weather conditions were forecasted to improve this week in Southern California, aiding firefighters in their battle against a wind-driven wildfire that's forced up to 20,000 people — including performers Cher, Dick Van Dyke and his wife — from their homes.
item.dc:creator: JAE C. HONG, ERIC THAYER and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump names former CIA official Ron Johnson as ambassador to Mexico
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday announced his one-time ambassador to El Salvador, Ron Johnson, would serve as ambassador to Mexico in his upcoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon federal judge temporarily blocks proposed Kroger-Albertsons merger
item.description: Oregon was one of several states to join the Federal Trade Commission’s lawsuit that aimed to block the merger.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, NewsNation

item.title: House task force cites 'various failures' ahead of 'preventable' Trump shooting
item.description: The House select committee that investigated the two assassination attempts against President-elect Trump released a report on Tuesday that found the July 13 attack at Trump’s rally in Butler, Pa., was “preventable and should not have happened.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO struggles, shouts while entering courthouse
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO struggled with deputies and shouted while arriving for a court appearance in Pennsylvania a day after he was arrested at a McDonald’s and charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MARK SCOLFORO, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US wildlife officials plan to label monarch butterflies as threatened species
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service plans to add the butterfly to the threatened species list by the end of next year following an extensive public comment period.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: House advances annual defense policy bill, teeing up final vote
item.description: The House advanced the annual defense policy bill on Tuesday, teeing up the mammoth measure for a final vote later this week. The chamber voted 211-207 along party lines to adopt the rule — which governs debate for legislation — for the sprawling bill, formally opening up debate in the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden stakes out economic legacy at Brookings as Trump readies takeover
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday made the case for the strength of his economic legacy as he prepared to leave office next month, arguing President-elect Trump would be inheriting a desirable situation and warning of the risks of the incoming administration’s proposals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell receives medical attention after falling
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) received medical attention after falling following a Republican lunch meeting Tuesday. A spokesman for McConnell said the GOP leader tripped after the lunch. "He sustained a minor cut to the face and sprained his wrist.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Military analysts break down national security concerns, doubt hobbyists at play
item.description: Military drone experts tell Fox News that SUV-size drones over New Jersey are not likely hobbyists and a foreign adversary could be responsible.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daniel Penny hits NYC bar for post-acquittal celebration, lets lawyers do the talking
item.description: Daniel Penny was spotted at a post-acquittal celebration Monday after jurors found him not guilty in subway chokehold death of Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO struggles, shouts while entering courthouse
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO struggled with deputies and shouted while arriving for a court appearance in Pennsylvania a day after he was arrested at a McDonald’s and charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Surgeon general pick accidentally set off gun, killing dad: Report
item.description: Nesheiwat has publicly referenced her father's death multiple times, though she has never mentioned her connection to the accident.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey becomes latest state to prohibit bans on books in school, public libraries
item.description: PRINCETON, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy signed a law Monday to prohibit public and school libraries from banning books in the state and to enshrine protections against civil and criminal charges for librarians who comply with the law.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Party trays, meal kits sold in Utah recalled over cucumber-related Salmonella concerns
item.description: Multiple companies have issued recalls of products sold across the nation and in Utah after potential exposure to Salmonella through now-recalled cucumbers.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO was likely killed with a ghost gun that can be made at home
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The brazen killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO was likely carried out with a ghost gun, one of the nearly untraceable weapons that can be made a home, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: La Linea, Sinaloa cartel unleash violence in Chihuahua
item.description: Mexican authorities say they have “restored order” in parts of the state of Chihuahua where organized criminal gangs engaged in violent confrontations in the past few days.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Amazon offering $25 flights for Prime members — but only for one eligible group
item.description: The deal, which runs through Friday, aims to offer 1,000 bookings per day for a total of 5,000 $25 fares.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect arrested at Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: UPenn graduate Luigi Mangione, 26, had a manifesto criticizing the healthcare industry when he was taken into questioning in connection with Brian Thompson's murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daniel Penny acquitted in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Polygamist with 20 'wives' to be sentenced for sex acts with children
item.description: A polygamous religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives ” including 10 underage girls faces decades in prison at his sentencing on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Scottie Scheffler ends his big year in the Bahamas with his 9th victory
item.description:
Scottie Scheffler returned from a two-month break and nothing changed. He won the Hero World Challenge on Sunday with a 9-under 63 to tie the tournament record at Albany Golf Club and win by six shots.
Scheffler, the first player since Tiger Woods in 2009 to spend an entire calendar year at No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tua Tagovailoa's TD pass to Jonnu Smith gives Dolphins 32-26 overtime win over Aaron Rodgers, Jets
item.description: Tagovailoa sealed it with a 10-yard touchdown pass to Jonnu Smith in overtime to help the Dolphins spoil Rodgers' first 300-yard passing game in nearly three years and beat the Jets for the ninth straight time in Miami.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: President-elect Trump's first sit-down interview since winning a second White House term aired Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Woman, 52, accused of robbing Billie Eilish fans at knifepoint near Oregon arena
item.description: A woman was arrested for allegedly stabbing a man, then threatening and robbing a couple of their sleeping bag near the Moda Center Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, ABC 4

item.title: Hemingway look-alikes visit Cuba and some of the late writer's favorite places
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Ernest Hemingway has returned to Cuba in a way. Eighteen white-bearded men who resemble the late U.S. author arrived in Havana to visit some of his favorite places when he lived on the island decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared in Syria in 2012, believed to be alive: Biden
item.description: “We think we can get him back," Biden told reporters at the White House, while acknowledging that “we have no direct evidence” of his status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Forever chemicals tainting food supply, destroying American farmers
item.description: Advocates like Erin Brockovich are sounding the alarm. She says when it comes to forever chemicals, “the storm is here.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio bill would require high-demand liquor bottles to be opened when sold
item.description: The bill aims to combat "flippers" who quickly buy up the limited supply of highly sought-after spirits in Ohio to illegally resell them on a secondary market for significantly more than what they paid.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, Fox 5

item.title: Lando Norris wins season-ending Abu Dhabi Grand Prix to seal constructors' title for McLaren
item.description: Lando Norris ended McLaren's 26-year wait for the Formula 1 constructors' title as he won the season-ending Abu Dhabi Grand Prix on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow, Russian state media say
item.description: Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, putting an end to the Assad family's 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New photos released of suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: The manhunt continues for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer as police released new photos of the suspect.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, Fox 5

item.title: Biden adds to the nation's list of national monuments during his term. There's an appetite for more
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. President Theodore Roosevelt did in 1906 what Congress was unwilling to do through legislation: He used his new authority under the Antiquities Act to designate Devils Tower in Wyoming as the first national monument.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
